strengths: The recurring job is concrete enough to anchor the package.
anchor_sentence: Apply an explicit simplicity constraint across software planning, implementation, refactoring, and read-only review so the agent produces the smallest correct change that follows project conventions and remains verifiable. Primary output: A minimal plan, implementation, refactor, or review followed by a concise handoff containing actual scope, complexity tradeoff, before and after verification, database check, and unresolved risks.. Exclusions: generic software development requests without an explicit simplicity signal, strict red-green-refactor TDD workflows, architecture design or large migrations that need dedicated system-level reasoning, performance diagnosis and security audit workflows, mechanical simplification that would violate correctness, safety, project conventions, public interfaces, or transaction semantics.
recommended_action: Intent is clear enough to package the first routeable version.

context.job: Apply an explicit simplicity constraint across software planning, implementation, refactoring, and read-only review so the agent produces the smallest correct change that follows project conventions and remains verifiable.
real_inputs: a feature, bug fix, or refactoring request that explicitly asks for a minimal or straightforward solution
existing repository code, project conventions, public interfaces, acceptance criteria, and relevant tests
a plan or diff that needs review for over-abstraction, unnecessary scope, control-flow complexity, or N+1 risk
database workflows where batching must be weighed against transaction, locking, streaming, memory, or rate-limit semantics
context.primary_output: A minimal plan, implementation, refactor, or review followed by a concise handoff containing actual scope, complexity tradeoff, before and after verification, database check, and unresolved risks.
context.description: Apply simple development constraints when the user explicitly asks for minimal or straightforward work, fewer abstractions, files, or jumps, flatter control flow, lightweight test-first changes, or N+1 avoidance. Use across planning, implementation, refactoring, and code review. Follow existing project conventions; do not replace dedicated TDD, architecture design, performance diagnosis, security review, or migration workflows.
exclusions: generic software development requests without an explicit simplicity signal
architecture design or large migrations that need dedicated system-level reasoning
performance diagnosis and security audit workflows
mechanical simplification that would violate correctness, safety, project conventions, public interfaces, or transaction semantics
constraints: correctness, safety, explicit requirements, project conventions, and acceptance criteria take priority over simplicity preferences
keep initial Skill context within the 700-token scaffold budget even at library maturity
do not add abstractions or files without a project convention, repeated responsibility, clear complexity reduction, or necessary test boundary
do not fabricate test, runtime, database, provider, human-review, or native-client evidence
publish one canonical Agent Skills package and represent unsupported platform behavior with explicit degradation notes
standards: trigger evaluation must cover explicit positives, generic negatives, specialist near-neighbors, Chinese requests, and four target invocation styles
recorded output fixtures must beat their baseline assertions while remaining labeled as fixtures
OpenAI, Claude, generic Agent Skills, and VS Code targets must pass structural conformance and package installation simulation
public beta release assets must include Apache-2.0, version parity, checksums, package verification, compatibility evidence, and a review expiry
provider-backed execution and native Claude or VS Code smoke tests remain missing evidence until actually observed
context.correction: The package evolved from a personal Codex-only constraint into a public Apache-2.0 Agent Skills library while preserving one neutral source and a strict 700-token initial context budget.
user_references: the user-approved simple-dev development boundary plan
the user-approved four-target public Apache-2.0 release plan
official Codex, Claude Code, VS Code Agent Skills, and agentskills.io installation conventions
